summary: PR #813 introduced an API v1 distributed relay bridge that posted plaintext OpenAI-style messages through relay-owned state, violating the relay-blind E2EE invariant for distributed inference.
impact: Code-level exposure risk existed for distributed API v1 traffic because relay state could carry plaintext prompts/messages. This was treated as a security incident; there is no confirmed exploitation in repository evidence.
fix: Prompt 1 fail-closed unsafe distributed API v1 relay dispatch (PR #834), Prompt 2 restored distributed API v1 relay through an E2EE envelope path (PR #836), and Prompt 3 added permanent anti-regression safeguards (PR #842).
lessons: Distributed relay paths must preserve ciphertext-only relay ownership at every boundary. Registration/auth hardening alone (for example PR #831) is insufficient without end-to-end relay-blind E2EE contract enforcement and invariant tests.
